Locate underpriced listings in the local store.

For every (make, model, year-band, mileage-band) cohort, computes the
median price across the synced corpus and lists current ads whose
price is at or below threshold × median. Cohorts smaller than
--min-samples (default 4) are skipped — comparing to a one-listing
median makes no sense.

The vehicle-specific filters (--make, --model) are optional. When
omitted, every make/model is considered separately. When set, the
comparison set is restricted to that filter — e.g. "find underpriced
Volvo XC70s" vs "find underpriced cars overall".

Usage:
  blocket-pp-cli arbitrage [flags]

Examples:
  blocket-pp-cli arbitrage --vertical car --make Volvo --threshold 0.8 --json

Flags:
  -h, --help              help for arbitrage
      --make string       Restrict to a single vehicle make.
      --min-samples int   Skip cohorts with fewer than this many comparable listings. (default 4)
      --model string      Restrict to a single vehicle model.
      --threshold float   Price as fraction of cohort median (0.8 = 20% under). (default 0.8)
      --vertical string   Vertical to scan (car, ads, boats, motorcycles). (default "car")

Filter ads in the local store by an arbitrary lat/lon and radius
in kilometres.

The Blocket public search API filters by Swedish region IDs (län), not
arbitrary lat/lon. The per-ad coordinates only become useful in the
local store. Sync ads first, then narrow with this command.

Usage:
  blocket-pp-cli geo near [flags]

Examples:
  blocket-pp-cli geo near --lat 59.33 --lon 18.06 --radius 30 --vertical car --json

Flags:

      --lon float         Center longitude.
      --radius float      Radius in kilometres. (default 30)

Import data from a JSONL file by issuing POST requests for each record.
Each line must be a valid JSON object. Failed records are logged to stderr
but do not stop the import.

  blocket-pp-cli import <resource> --input data.jsonl

  # Dry-run to preview without sending

  # Import from stdin
  cat data.jsonl | blocket-pp-cli import <resource> --input -

Sync data from the API into a local SQLite database. Supports resumable
incremental sync (only fetches new data since last sync) and full resync.
Once synced, use the 'search' command for instant full-text search.

Exit codes & warnings:
  Resources the API denies access to (HTTP 403, or HTTP 400 with an
  access-policy body) are reported as warnings rather than failing the
  run. In --json mode each is emitted as a {"event":"sync_warning",...}
  line carrying status, reason, and message fields, and a final
  {"event":"sync_summary",...} aggregates the run.

  Exit 0 when at least one resource synced and no resource flagged in
  the spec as critical (x-critical: true) failed; non-critical failures
  emit {"event":"sync_warning","reason":"exit_policy_default_changed",
  ...} so callers can detect that a partial failure was tolerated. Pass
  --strict to exit non-zero on any per-resource failure. Exit is always
  non-zero when every selected resource failed, regardless of --strict.

  # Sync all resources

  # Sync specific resources only
  blocket-pp-cli sync --resources channels,messages

  # Full resync (ignore previous checkpoint)
  blocket-pp-cli sync --full

  # Incremental sync: only records from the last 7 days
  blocket-pp-cli sync --since 7d

  # Parallel sync with 8 workers
  blocket-pp-cli sync --concurrency 8

  # Latest-only: refresh head of each resource, no historical backfill
  blocket-pp-cli sync --latest-only
